nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent instruction file providing comprehensive project overview including pipeline stages, crawl engines, translation backends, commands, config files, environment variables, and tech stack for the novel2epub tool.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Condensed AI agent instruction file covering the novel2epub project pipeline, key commands, architecture components, technical notes, and tech stack for Claude and similar agents.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: User-facing documentation in Vietnamese covering installation, configuration with config.example.yaml, translation backends (CLI AI, Google, none), CLI usage, Web UI, environment variables, and recommended workflow for novel2epub.

nodes.name: config.example.yaml
nodes.summary: Annotated configuration template defining all novel2epub settings: novel metadata (title, author, slug), crawl engine options (http/crawl4ai with selectors and pagination), AI translation backend with CLI prompt template, glossary, and output paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese comments explain each config section with detailed inline guidance for Chinese web novel crawling and AI translation setup.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python package dependency declarations listing 11 runtime libraries for HTTP crawling (requests, beautifulsoup4), EPUB generation (ebooklib), AI translation (deep-translator), YAML config parsing (PyYAML, ruamel.yaml), web UI (fastapi, uvicorn, jinja2, python-multipart), and testing (pytest).

nodes.name: sources.yaml
nodes.summary: Site-specific crawl preset library defining engine types, CSS content/toc selectors, pagination rules, and encoding for 9 Chinese web novel sources including biquge, sto9, aixdzs, qidian, 69shuba, shuqi, qqread, baike, and shuhaige.

edges.label: README directs users to copy config.example.yaml for setup
both are AI agent instruction documents for the same project
AGENTS.md Source Presets section explains sources.yaml usage
CLAUDE.md Technical Notes section lists sources.yaml among config files
